purpose: Same-scene test with V7-063 prompt format (Continuation prefix)
hypothesis: If Continuation prefix fixes audio, same-scene should work
conclusion: Seedance extend audio duplication is scene-dependent, NOT prompt-format-dependent. When extending in same scene, later segments (seg2→seg3) replicate audio from input video. The 'Continuation' prefix does not prevent this.
insight: Continuation prefix helped seg1→seg2 (0.84→0.04) but NOT seg2→seg3 (0.95→0.88). The bug accumulates along the extend chain.
recommendation: This is a Seedance API behavior, not a pipeline bug. Workarounds: (1) Different scenes per segment, (2) Strip audio from input video before extending, (3) Investigate if Seedance has a parameter to force new audio generation
next_test: Try stripping audio from seg2.mp4 before feeding to extend for seg3 — if seg3 gets independent audio, the root cause is confirmed: Seedance extend copies input audio track
